surface.name: Dagster OSS quickstart first local ETL pipeline
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation home presents Build your first Dagster pipeline as its general quickstart. That guide selects Dagster OSS for local development, creates one asset containing a complete CSV extract, transform, and load operation, runs it from the Dagster UI, and explicitly congratulates the developer for building and running a first pipeline.

alternatives_considered.name: Dagster+ Serverless quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The OSS quickstart is the general next page from the documentation home and does not require a Dagster+ account or hosted project.

alternatives_considered.name: Dagster+ Hybrid deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The quickstart makes Hybrid deployment optional after the local pipeline has run.

alternatives_considered.name: Dagster basics tutorial
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The longer tutorial is a next step after the bounded quickstart's first pipeline.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without a Dagster account or project, install the local prerequisites, scaffold the official quickstart project, define its sample ETL asset, materialize it in the local Dagster UI, and verify the transformed CSV.
assumed_prior_state: No Dagster account, Dagster installation, project, virtual environment, asset definition, sample data, pipeline run, or cloud deployment is assumed.
A supported Mac, Windows, or Linux computer, terminal, browser, text editor, network connection, and permission to install local software are available.
No installation, project scaffolding, code editing, local server start, or materialization was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Build and run your first pipeline with Dagster.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The official local project materializes its `processed_data` ETL asset and writes the transformed sample rows, including the new `age_group` column, to `processed_data.csv`.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Running `cat src/dagster_quickstart/defs/data/processed_data.csv` shows the documented header `id,name,age,city,age_group` and the four transformed rows ending in Young, Middle, Senior, and Middle.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The guide is titled Build your first Dagster pipeline and ends the local run and verification sequence with 'Congratulations! You've just built and run your first pipeline with Dagster.'

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The materialized asset and verified output are the guide's first complete data-pipeline result. Production deployment, additional assets, automation, testing, and observability are later steps.

prerequisites.requirement: Install Python 3.10 or higher; the installation guide recommends Python 3.13.
Install the `uv` Python package manager for the recommended quickstart branch; installing it also provides `uvx`.
Be able to use a terminal, browser, and text editor to run commands and create the documented CSV and Python files.
Allow the scaffold and dependency commands to download the current Dagster tooling, project dependencies, and pandas.

primary_path.action: Open the Dagster documentation home and follow the Quickstart link to Build your first Dagster pipeline.
details: The home page describes the quickstart as the way to build a first Dagster pipeline.
The selected guide uses Dagster OSS for local development.
primary_path.output: The official local quickstart and its prerequisites are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide begins with Step 1: Scaffold a new Dagster project.

primary_path.action: Install Python 3.10 or higher and install `uv` with the operating-system command shown in the Dagster installation guide.
details: Mac and Linux use `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh`.
Windows uses the documented PowerShell installer command.
primary_path.input: A supported computer with terminal and installation permission
primary_path.output: Python and the recommended `uv` and `uvx` commands are available.
primary_path.success_signal: The environment can invoke `uvx` for the next scaffold command.

primary_path.action: Run `uvx create-dagster@latest project dagster-quickstart` and answer `y` when prompted to run `uv sync`.
details: The command scaffolds the project and the accepted prompt creates its virtual environment and installs dependencies.
primary_path.output: A `dagster-quickstart` project with `pyproject.toml`, source package, definitions, tests, and lock file is created.
primary_path.success_signal: The generated project has the structure displayed by the guide.

primary_path.action: Change to `dagster-quickstart`, activate `.venv` with the operating-system command, and run `uv add pandas`.
primary_path.output: The project's virtual environment is active and pandas is added.
primary_path.success_signal: The environment provides `dg` and the project can import pandas.

primary_path.output: An asset-definition file is scaffolded in the project.

primary_path.action: Create `src/dagster_quickstart/defs/data/sample_data.csv` and copy the guide's four-row sample dataset into it.
details: Create the directory and file with `mkdir src/dagster_quickstart/defs/data && touch src/dagster_quickstart/defs/data/sample_data.csv`.
The input columns are `id,name,age,city` for Alice, Bob, Charlie, and Diana.
primary_path.input: The exact sample CSV shown in the quickstart
primary_path.output: The source CSV exists at the path used by the asset.
primary_path.success_signal: The file contains the documented header and four rows.

primary_path.action: Replace `assets.py` with the guide's `processed_data` asset definition.
details: The code reads the sample CSV with pandas, adds `age_group` using bins and labels, writes `processed_data.csv`, and returns `Data loaded successfully`.
primary_path.output: The project defines one asset representing the entire ETL operation.
primary_path.success_signal: The file contains the documented `processed_data` asset definition and its input and output paths.

primary_path.action: From the project root, run `dg dev` and open `http://localhost:3000`.
details: The command starts the local Dagster development service and UI.
primary_path.output: The local Dagster UI displays the project.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser shows the Dagster UI and its Assets tab.

primary_path.action: Open Assets, click View lineage, and click Materialize.
details: The guide uses the lineage page's Materialize action to run the pipeline.
primary_path.input: The `processed_data` asset in the local UI
primary_path.output: Dagster launches a materialization run for the ETL asset.
primary_path.success_signal: A run is available from the Runs tab.

primary_path.action: Open the Runs tab and click View for the executing run.
details: The run view exposes the execution of the materialization selected by the guide.
primary_path.output: The developer can inspect the run in Dagster's UI.

details: The guide says the output should contain the added `age_group` column and the four transformed rows.
primary_path.output: The transformed CSV is printed in the terminal.
primary_path.success_signal: The output matches the documented header and Young, Middle, Senior, and Middle values, establishing the first pipeline result.

branches.path: Install `uv` with the documented PowerShell command and activate the generated environment with `.venv\Scripts\activate`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes installation and activation syntax only.

branches.condition: The developer uses Mac or Linux
branches.path: Install `uv` with the documented shell installer and activate with `source .venv/bin/activate`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses the Unix shell commands shown in the guide.

branches.condition: The developer uses pip instead of recommended `uv`
branches.path: Install `create-dagster`, scaffold the project, create and activate `.venv`, install pandas, and install the project editable with the documented pip commands; pip earlier than 25.1 requires an upgrade or separate CLI installation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds manual environment and editable-project installation before the same asset steps.

branches.path: Run `dg list defs` to see `processed_data`, then `dg check defs` to load and validate definitions.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an optional definition check before starting the UI.

branches.condition: The developer runs from the CLI instead of the UI
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces UI materialization with the documented CLI launch while keeping file verification.

friction_gates.description: The local route requires Python 3.10 or higher and the selected package-manager tooling.
Installation and virtual-environment activation commands differ between Windows and Mac or Linux.
Project scaffolding and `uv sync` install the generated project's dependencies.
The developer must create the exact sample data file and Python asset at the paths used by the published code.
The developer must run the local service and open its browser UI at localhost.

sources.title: Build your first Dagster pipeline | Dagster Docs
sections_used: Step 1: Scaffold a new Dagster project

sources.title: Installing Dagster | Dagster Docs
sections_used: Installation requirements for using the create-dagster CLI

excluded_after_success: Production deployment to Dagster OSS or Dagster+ Hybrid, cloud accounts, additional assets and dependencies, schedules, sensors, automation, tests, asset checks, debugging, observability, and CI/CD occur after the verified local pipeline.
